prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(claim_item_for_staging secondary_slot_offhand)
(reserve_stash_item_for_entry secondary_slot_offhand stash_wood_plank)
(assign_item_type_to_entry secondary_slot_offhand item_type_standard)
(mark_entry_ready secondary_slot_offhand item_type_standard)
(mark_secondary_material_for_slot secondary_slot_offhand secondary_material_buckle item_type_standard)
(release_item_type_from_entry secondary_slot_offhand item_type_standard)
(attach_tool_to_entry secondary_slot_offhand assembly_tool)
(confirm_secondary_material_with_tool secondary_slot_offhand secondary_material_buckle assembly_tool)
(release_tool_from_entry secondary_slot_offhand assembly_tool)
(claim_item_for_staging primary_slot_main)
(reserve_stash_item_for_entry primary_slot_main stash_iron_ingot)
(assign_item_type_to_entry primary_slot_main item_type_standard)
(mark_entry_ready primary_slot_main item_type_standard)
(mark_primary_material_for_slot primary_slot_main primary_material_metal_core item_type_standard)
(attach_tool_to_entry primary_slot_main assembly_tool)
(confirm_primary_material_with_tool primary_slot_main primary_material_metal_core assembly_tool)
(release_tool_from_entry primary_slot_main assembly_tool)
(assemble_prepared_item primary_slot_main secondary_slot_offhand primary_material_metal_core secondary_material_buckle prepared_item_alpha)
(validate_prepared_item prepared_item_alpha primary_slot_main item_type_standard)
(activate_prepared_item_in_primary_slot primary_slot_main prepared_item_alpha)
(activate_prepared_item_in_secondary_slot secondary_slot_offhand prepared_item_alpha)
(release_item_type_from_entry primary_slot_main item_type_standard)
(claim_item_for_staging loadout_alpha)
(reserve_stash_item_for_entry loadout_alpha stash_leather_strip)
(assign_item_type_to_entry loadout_alpha item_type_standard)
(mark_entry_ready loadout_alpha item_type_standard)
(attach_upgrade_module_to_loadout loadout_alpha upgrade_module_alpha prepared_item_alpha)
(prepare_loadout_for_module_integration loadout_alpha upgrade_module_alpha prepared_item_alpha item_type_standard)
(apply_repair_kit_to_loadout loadout_alpha repair_kit_standard)
(attach_tool_to_entry loadout_alpha assembly_tool)
(confirm_module_integration_without_secondary_consumption loadout_alpha repair_kit_standard assembly_tool upgrade_module_alpha prepared_item_alpha)
(install_power_core_on_loadout loadout_alpha power_core_mk1)
(finalize_module_integration loadout_alpha power_core_mk1 upgrade_module_alpha prepared_item_alpha)
(finalize_loadout_without_component loadout_alpha)
(release_item_type_from_entry loadout_alpha item_type_standard)
(release_tool_from_entry loadout_alpha assembly_tool)
(remove_repair_kit_from_loadout loadout_alpha repair_kit_standard)
(remove_power_core_from_loadout loadout_alpha power_core_mk1)
(assign_item_type_to_entry secondary_slot_offhand item_type_standard)
(apply_preservation_kit_to_item secondary_slot_offhand preservation_kit_standard item_type_standard)
(activate_secondary_slot_from_stash secondary_slot_offhand stash_wood_plank preservation_kit_standard)
(release_item_type_from_entry secondary_slot_offhand item_type_standard)
(claim_item_for_staging loadout_beta)
(reserve_stash_item_for_entry loadout_beta stash_wood_plank)
(assign_item_type_to_entry loadout_beta item_type_standard)
(mark_entry_ready loadout_beta item_type_standard)
(attach_upgrade_module_to_loadout loadout_beta upgrade_module_beta prepared_item_alpha)
(prepare_loadout_for_module_integration loadout_beta upgrade_module_beta prepared_item_alpha item_type_standard)
(apply_repair_kit_to_loadout loadout_beta repair_kit_standard)
(attach_tool_to_entry loadout_beta assembly_tool)
(confirm_module_integration_without_secondary_consumption loadout_beta repair_kit_standard assembly_tool upgrade_module_beta prepared_item_alpha)
(install_power_core_on_loadout loadout_beta power_core_mk1)
(finalize_module_integration loadout_beta power_core_mk1 upgrade_module_beta prepared_item_alpha)
(finalize_loadout_without_component loadout_beta)
(release_item_type_from_entry loadout_beta item_type_standard)
(release_tool_from_entry loadout_beta assembly_tool)
(remove_repair_kit_from_loadout loadout_beta repair_kit_standard)
(remove_power_core_from_loadout loadout_beta power_core_mk1)
(assign_item_type_to_entry loadout_beta item_type_standard)
(apply_preservation_kit_to_item loadout_beta preservation_kit_standard item_type_standard)
(activate_loadout_from_stash loadout_beta stash_wood_plank preservation_kit_standard)
(assign_item_type_to_entry primary_slot_main item_type_standard)
(apply_preservation_kit_to_item primary_slot_main preservation_kit_standard item_type_standard)
(activate_primary_slot_from_stash primary_slot_main stash_iron_ingot preservation_kit_standard)
(assign_item_type_to_entry loadout_alpha item_type_standard)
(apply_preservation_kit_to_item loadout_alpha preservation_kit_standard item_type_standard)
(activate_loadout_from_stash loadout_alpha stash_leather_strip preservation_kit_standard)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for "stash_to_active_inventory_rotation". I need to output only the sequence of ground actions that solve it, each on its own line in the form (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Examining Problem Details  
- The task is a planning problem in PDDL, specifically for "stash_to_active_inventory_rotation."  
- The goal is to output a sequence of ground actions, each on a new line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).  

## Planning Solution Approach  
- Focusing on generating the correct sequence of actions to solve the inventory rotation problem.  
- Ensuring each action follows the required PDDL format for clear execution.

## Examining Problem Details  
- Available items include standard preservation kits, repair kits, objective tags for stealth and assault, and power cores like power_core_mk1.  
- Initial facts show stash items, tools, consumables, and reserved materials for primary and secondary slots, with prepared_item_alpha shared across loadouts alpha and beta.  
- Loadouts alpha and beta have the same prepared_item_alpha, which may cause issues if used simultaneously, but this needs further exploration.  

## Exploring Available Resources  
- Loadouts have upgrade modules (alpha for loadout_alpha, beta for loadout_beta) and access to cosmetics, special components, and other kits.  
- Primary and secondary slots are part of equipment_slot_group, while loadouts are loadout_group, both classified as item_entry for activation.  
- Materials for slots are already reserved, such as primary_material_metal_core for primary_slot_main and secondary_material_buckle for secondary_slot_offhand.  

## Planning Activation Process  
- Activating items involves claiming, reserving from stash, assigning type, and staging, with additional steps like preparing materials for slots.  
- For slots, activation requires actions like activate_primary_slot_from_stash, needing entity_activation_marked, entity_reserved_from_stash, and entity_bound_to_preservation_kit.  
- Loadouts and slots share resources like primary_slot_main, which may be shared between loadout_alpha and loadout_beta, potentially affecting activation steps.

## Evaluating Resource Constraints  
- Only one item_type_standard is available, but multiple entities (two slots and two loadouts) need it for activation.  
- Assigning item_type_standard to an entity consumes its availability, requiring release to reassign, which complicates simultaneous staging.  

## Assessing Activation Conflicts  
- Both primary_slot_main and secondary_slot_offhand need item_type_standard assigned to be staged and prepared, but this can't happen simultaneously with one type.  
- Preparing materials and confirming slots for prepared_item assembly requires all entities to be staged with assigned type, creating a bottleneck.

## Examining Slot Preparation Challenges
- Preparing both primary and secondary slots for assembly requires them to be ready and confirmed simultaneously, but this conflicts with limited item_type availability.
- Assigning item_type to one slot consumes it, preventing assignment to the other slot until released, creating a bottleneck for simultaneous staging.

## Investigating Validation Requirements
- Validating the prepared item after assembly requires primary_slot to still have entity_assigned_type, but releasing the type for the second slot breaks this condition.
- The process gets stuck as preparing one slot fully (confirmed, ready) requires holding onto the type, making it impossible to prepare the other slot without violating validation needs.

## Investigating Confirmation Pathways  
- Both tool and consumable methods for confirming slots require assigned_type at some point, typically during staging or material preparation.  
- Confirming with a tool (confirm_primary_material_with_tool) needs the slot staged, prepared, and with a tool attached, but doesn't require type for the final confirmation step.  

## Analyzing Type Release Possibilities  
- Releasing item_type after confirming one slot could allow assigning it to another, but this breaks validation needs for the prepared item.  
- The process requires both slots to be ready and confirmed simultaneously, creating a bottleneck with limited item_type availability.

## Exploring Type Release Strategy  
- After marking a slot as prepared, releasing the item_type becomes possible, making it available again for another slot.  
- This release allows assigning the type to the second slot, enabling sequential preparation without violating resource limits.  

## Examining Tool-Based Confirmation  
- Confirming with a tool doesn't require the assigned_type, only that the slot is prepared, staged, and has a tool attached.  
- The sequence involves claiming a slot, reserving materials, assigning and then releasing the type, and finally confirming with the tool, keeping both slots ready and confirmed.  

## Assessing Consumable Allocation Challenges  
- Using consumables for confirmation requires finalizing allocation, which needs assigned_type, creating a similar bottleneck as with tools.  
- Allocating consumables sets the slot as ready but not confirmed, requiring late type assignment to finalize, complicating simultaneous preparation.  

## Investigating Resource Reuse  
- After preparing and confirming one slot, releasing the tool might be needed, but the ready and confirmed status persists.  
- This suggests preparing slots sequentially is feasible, reusing limited resources like tools and consumables for both slots.

## Validating Prepared Items  
- Validating prepared_item_alpha for primary_slot_main requires it to be ready and assigned item_type_standard, which is currently satisfied.  
- After validation, prepared_item_alpha is marked as validated, enabling further steps like slot activation.  

## Checking Slot Activation Requirements  
- Activating prepared_item_alpha in both primary_slot_main and secondary_slot_offhand requires readiness, confirmation, and validation, all currently met.  
- The activation process sets entity_ready_for_activation for both slots, using prepared_item_alpha without consuming it.  

## Managing Item Type Assignments  
- Item_type_standard is currently assigned to primary_slot_main, needed for validation, but must be released afterward for loadout processing.  
- Loadouts and later steps like applying preservation kits require entity_assigned_type, creating a resource management challenge with limited item_type availability.  

## Processing Loadouts  
- Loadouts alpha and beta share slots and prepared_item_alpha but differ in upgrade_modules, requiring careful resource handling.  
- Processing a loadout, like loadout_alpha, involves staging and assigning item_type, but may need releasing type from slots first to avoid conflicts.

## Extending Loadout Processing  
- Loadouts like alpha and beta share prepared_item_alpha, allowing both to be staged simultaneously if resources permit, but limited type availability requires sequential handling.  
- Processing a loadout fully to entity_ready_for_activation involves staging, assigning type, and using resources like tools, but must release them before activating to reuse for the next loadout.  

## Optimizing Resource Reuse  
- Stash items and preservation kits return to the stash after activation, enabling sequential processing where one entity is fully activated before moving to the next.  
- This approach ensures resources like item_type_standard and preservation_kit_standard are reused, avoiding conflicts when preparing multiple slots or loadouts.  

## Managing Slot Activation  
- Both primary_slot_main and secondary_slot_offhand must be ready, confirmed, and validated with prepared_item_alpha before activating, setting entity_ready_for_activation on each.  
- Activating slots requires applying preservation_kit_standard, which needs assigned_type on the slot, necessitating type reassignment during this step due to earlier release.

## Handling Loadout Stash Items  
- Loadout_alpha reserves stash_leather_strip, as primary and secondary slots already reserved other stash_items, leaving leather_strip available.  
- Upon activating loadout_alpha, its reserved stash_item returns to the stash, enabling reuse for subsequent loadouts like loadout_beta.  

## Assigning Item Types for Loadouts  
- Loadout_alpha requires item_type_standard for staging and prepare_integration, assigned before marking entry ready.  
- After prepare_integration sets integration_ready, item_type_standard can be released, as it’s no longer needed for loadout steps.  

## Processing Loadout Steps  
- Loadout_alpha involves claiming, staging, and attaching resources like repair_kits and tools, all requiring entity_staged but not item_type.  
- Finalizing loadout_alpha sets entity_ready_for_activation, involving steps like installing power_cores and confirming module integration without secondary consumption.  

## Managing Resource Release  
- After finalizing loadout_alpha, resources like repair_kits and tools remain attached, requiring removal (e.g., remove_repair_kit_from_loadout) for reuse in loadout_beta.  
- Upgrade_modules, once installed, have no remove action, suggesting they stay with the loadout, impacting resource availability for subsequent processing.

## Activating Loadout Components  
- Primary_slot_main activates using stash_iron_ingot and preservation_kit_standard, returning iron to the stash and making preservation_kit available.  
- Loadout_alpha activates with stash_leather_strip and preservation_kit_standard, returning leather to the stash and freeing the preservation_kit.  

## Managing Stash Item Assignments  
- Secondary_slot_offhand uses stash_wood_plank, which returns to the stash after activation for reuse in loadout_beta.  
- Loadout_beta reserves stash_wood_plank after secondary activation, ensuring sequential processing without conflicts.  

## Ensuring Process Requirements  
- Actions like prepare_integration require assigned_type, so item_type_standard must remain assigned until after staging and integration steps.  
- Entity_staged status is necessary throughout loadout processing, including attaching upgrades and preparing integration.  

## Handling Unused Features  
- Objective_tag_assault for loadout_beta and stealth for loadout_alpha were available but not used, as they weren’t required for activation.  
- No consumables were used in slot processing, with assembly_tool and prepared_item_alpha remaining unconsumed, confirming standard assembly.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
